name: get_reply_empty_output_when_should_send
code:
proof fn phi_1_get_reply_empty_output_when_should_send(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState,
    src: AbstractEndPoint, seqno: nat, k: AbstractKey,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>, m: Message, out: Set<Packet>,
)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_get_request_reply(pre, post, src, seqno, k, sm, m, out, true),
        valid_key(k),
    ensures
        out == Set::<Packet>::empty(),
{
}
reason: A successful get-request reply (should_send=true) must emit exactly one packet; an empty output set would mean the response is silently dropped

name: send_exceeds_max_seqno_still_sends
code:
proof fn phi_2_send_exceeds_max_seqno_still_sends(
    pre: SingleDelivery<Message>, post: SingleDelivery<Message>,
    m: Message, dst: AbstractEndPoint,
    sm: Option<SingleMessage<Message>>,
    params: AbstractParameters,
)
    requires
        SingleDelivery::send_single_message(pre, post, m, dst, sm, params),
        ack_state_lookup(dst, pre.send_state).num_packets_acked
          + ack_state_lookup(dst, pre.send_state).un_acked.len() + 1 > params.max_seqno,
    ensures
        sm is Some,
{
}
reason: When the sequence number exceeds max_seqno the protocol must suppress sending; allowing Some(sm) would violate the flow-control bound

name: redirect_modifies_received_requests
code:
proof fn phi_3_redirect_modifies_received_requests(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState,
    src: AbstractEndPoint, seqno: nat, k: AbstractKey,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>, m: Message, out: Set<Packet>,
)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_get_request_reply(pre, post, src, seqno, k, sm, m, out, true),
        valid_key(k),
        pre.delegation_map[k] != pre.constants.me,
    ensures
        post.received_requests != pre.received_requests,
{
}
reason: A redirected request is not served locally, so received_requests must stay unchanged; mutation here would miscount application-level requests

name: lookup_none_despite_key_present
code:
proof fn phi_4_lookup_none_despite_key_present(h: Hashtable, k: AbstractKey)
    requires
        h.dom().contains(k),
    ensures
        hashtable_lookup(h, k) is None,
{
}
reason: Looking up a key known to be in the hashtable must return Some; returning None would lose stored data and break get-request correctness

name: get_request_changes_hashtable
code:
proof fn phi_5_get_request_changes_hashtable(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState,
    pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>,
)
    requires
        pkt.msg is Message,
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_get_request(pre, post, pkt, out),
    ensures
        post.h != pre.h,
{
}
reason: A read-only GetRequest must never mutate the hashtable; allowing h to change would turn a get into an implicit write
